notes: Alcohol can be brought into Australia within duty-free limits subject to customs declarations
Australia has strict biosecurity laws and many food items are restricted or prohibited
There is no limit on bringing cash into Australia, but amounts over AUD 10,000 must be declared.
Duty-free allowances apply when entering Australia, covering alcohol, tobacco, and other goods within set limits.
Personal electronics can be brought into Australia, but high-value items may be subject to duty.
Medication can be brought into Australia for personal use, but strict rules apply to controlled substances.
Tobacco can be brought into Australia within strict limits, with taxes applying to most quantities.
Most weapons are prohibited from being brought into Australia, with very strict import controls.
Prescription drugs can be brought into Australia for personal use, but strict documentation rules apply.
Travelers must declare many items when entering Australia, especially food, cash, and restricted goods.
